vertical_split_selection.sort_order: descending by non_pk_column_count, then table name

fragment_roles: rows where Scene > 6 or is NULL
rows where ParagraphNum > 2340 or is NULL

base_predicate_sqls: (((("Description" = 'a conspirator against Caesar' OR "Description" = 'servant to Brutus') OR ("Description" = 'friend to Antony' OR "Description" = 'friend to Caesar')) OR (("Description" = 'Nobleman of Scotland' OR "Description" = 'a follower of Cade') OR ("Description" = 'a Greek prince' OR "Description" = 'courtier'))) OR ("Description" IS NULL OR "Description" NOT IN ('', 'a conspirator against Caesar', 'friend to Antony', 'friend to Caesar', 'Nobleman of Scotland', 'a Greek prince', 'courtier', 'servant to Brutus', 'a follower of Cade')))
fragment_roles: rows where Description = a conspirator against Caesar + rows where Description = servant to Brutus + rows where Description = friend to Antony + rows where Description = friend to Caesar + rows where Description = Nobleman of Scotland + rows where Description = a follower of Cade + rows where Description = a Greek prince + rows where Description = courtier + rows where Description is NULL or not in {'', 'a conspirator against Caesar', 'friend to Antony', 'friend to Caesar', 'Nobleman of Scotland', 'a Greek prince', 'courtier', 'servant to Brutus', 'a follower of Cade'} (catch-all 'OTHER' bucket)
characters.fragment_role_B: rows where Description = a conspirator against Caesar + rows where Description = servant to Brutus + rows where Description = friend to Antony + rows where Description = friend to Caesar + rows where Description = Nobleman of Scotland + rows where Description = a follower of Cade + rows where Description = a Greek prince + rows where Description = courtier + rows where Description is NULL or not in {'', 'a conspirator against Caesar', 'friend to Antony', 'friend to Caesar', 'Nobleman of Scotland', 'a Greek prince', 'courtier', 'servant to Brutus', 'a follower of Cade'} (catch-all 'OTHER' bucket)

justification.cross_local_query_count_interpretation: Distinct cross-local workload queries where this fragment pair (different original tables) co-occurs; not an estimate of single-local queries after relocation.

relocation_engine_debug.pair_weight_meaning: Weights count co-occurrences of two fragments (from different original tables) inside workload queries that already span 2+ locals. They rank relocation candidates; they do not equal the number of queries that will become single-local after a move.
